final_answer: COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through close contact. The virus can spread through tiny droplets when an infected person coughs, sneezes, or talks. Infected individuals can spread the virus even if they have no symptoms. Droplets from an infected person's breath can land on nearby individuals. Close contact includes sitting next to someone or sharing a space. Risk of transmission increases in crowded spaces with poor airflow. Spending more than 30 minutes in close contact with an infected person raises risk.
